actual_coverage.reasoning: Both probe multi-year/durable growth drivers and the Perfecta roadmap versus pre-pandemic baseline.
Predicted splits Q2/Q3 headwinds but does not address Q4 confidence cadence specifically.
Directly asks Med booking velocity/remaining capacity and close-in window, aligning with damage/what’s left to book.
Lead-time/close-in mix question captures whether softness is near-term versus further out, similar to 'turning the corner' framing.
Asks for APD/yield uplift and ramp for Perfect Day Mexico/Cozumel and related destinations, i.e., the same ramp/economics topic.
Predicted focuses on destination economics; the actual asks a construction status update (pause/resume), which is not addressed.
Predicted asks sustainability of yield drivers into 2027, overlapping with whether 2024 Europe headwinds reverse in 2027.
Direct like-for-like versus new hardware/destination yield decomposition; same ask.
Operational mitigations to fuel costs (itinerary/speed) and cost posture overlap with itinerary-change/efficiency questions.
Hedging framework is covered, but the capacity threshold for NCC ex-fuel inflation is not asked in predictions.
Card/loyalty economics and impact on repeat/booking/spend directly target yield uplift from repeat guests/loyalty.
Touches air/flight impacts but not specifically North American airfare behavior or consumers’ ability to absorb it.

missed_actual_themes: Specific Q4 yield cadence/confidence to reach full-year guide despite Europe headwinds
Status of Mexico construction pause/resumption due to environmental review
Threshold for NCC ex-fuel inflation at higher capacity growth (2%-3%)
Impact of higher North American airfares on consumer behavior and demand
overpredicted_themes: Balance sheet financing, maturities and ECA structures
Deep AI/digital attribution and onboard pre-cruise margin mechanics
Detailed fleet orderbook/ICON economics and deployment mix minutiae
summary: Coverage was solid (8/12 actuals materially addressed), especially on yield decomposition, Mediterranean booking dynamics, Perfect Day Mexico ramp, hedging, costs timing vs structural, and loyalty/card impacts. Gaps included a Q4 yield cadence question, Mexico construction status, NCC ex-fuel inflation thresholds at higher capacity, and North American airfare effects. Precision was moderate (~59%); many overpredictions focused on capital returns, JV/TUI, financing, and AI attribution that did not appear in the actual Q&A.
